_comment: HELD-OUT ANTI-OVERFIT demonstrator. The BRIEF tells the model to prepare the Bell state |Phi+> whose Z-basis populations are 50/50 between |00> and |11>. The judge auto-grades the VISIBLE spec (the population distribution) at the reproducibility/performance gates, and HOLDS OUT the X-parity <X0X1> = +1 — which the model is never told. A circuit that matches the populations with the wrong relative phase (|Phi->) passes structure/reproducibility/performance but is REJECTED at the anti-overfit gate (exit 6). This is the held-out reference that gives exit 6 teeth.

observables.note: |Phi+> has <X0X1> = +1; a wrong-phase |Phi-> that matches the populations has -1
